name: Vaishno Devi Temple
description: Sacred cave temple dedicated to Mata Vaishno Devi housing three natural rock formations (pindis) of Lakshmi, Saraswati, and Kali. 13 km trek from Katra.

name: Bhairon Temple
description: Temple 2.5 km above the main shrine. Pilgrimage is considered incomplete without visiting Bhairon Baba. Offers panoramic Himalayan views.

name: Ardhkuwari Temple
description: Holy cave midway on the trek where Mata hid for 9 months. Pilgrims crawl through a narrow cave passage. Major rest point on the yatra.

name: Ban Ganga
description: First stop on the trek. Mata shot an arrow creating this river for her thirsty followers. Pilgrims wash hands and feet before the climb.

name: Katra Town
description: Base camp town for the Vaishno Devi pilgrimage. Bustling markets selling puja items, walking sticks, and local food. Starting point of trek.

name: Shiv Khori
description: Natural cave shrine with a 4-feet natural Shiva lingam. 150-meter cave passage with stalactite formations resembling Hindu deities.

name: Raghunath Temple
description: Largest temple complex in North India with 7 shrines. Houses rare Sanskrit manuscripts and Pahari miniature paintings. In Jammu city.

name: Bahu Fort
description: Ancient fort overlooking the Tawi River, believed to be built 3000 years ago. Houses a Kali temple and offers panoramic views of Jammu.

name: Amar Mahal Palace
description: 19th-century French-style palace perched on a hilltop. Now a museum with a 120 kg gold throne, Pahari paintings, and royal artifacts.

name: Patnitop
description: Hill station at 2024m with panoramic Himalayan views. Popular for paragliding, skiing, and pine forest walks. 2 hours from Katra.

name: Sanasar
description: Cup-shaped meadow surrounded by pine forests. Known as Jammu's adventure capital — paragliding, horse riding, and camping.

name: Mansar Lake
description: Sacred lake surrounded by hills and forests. Newlyweds traditionally visit for blessings. Rich in Hindu mythological significance.

name: Charan Paduka
description: Rock with Mata's holy footprints imprinted naturally. First major shrine on the trek, about 1.5 km from Katra. Pilgrims pay respects here.

name: Hathi Matha
description: Elephant-head shaped rock formation on the trek route. Natural viewpoint offering breathtaking valley views and a rest stop for pilgrims.

name: Sanjhi Chhat
description: Helicopter base on the trek route. Beautiful viewpoint at 6800 ft. Rest area with food stalls and stunning Trikuta mountain views.

name: Surinsar Lake
description: Twin lake paired with Mansar — together a Ramsar wetland site. Dedicated to Lord Shiva, surrounded by dense forests and hills.

name: Peer Kho Cave
description: Cave temple in Jammu dedicated to Lord Shiva. Houses a natural lingam formed by continuous water dripping. Ancient meditation site.

name: Dera Baba Banda
description: Sacred gurudwara near Katra associated with Sikh warrior-saint Banda Bahadur. Peaceful site with historical significance for Sikhs.

name: Jhajjar Kotli
description: Scenic picnic spot on the Jammu-Katra highway with waterfalls and a river crossing. Perfect stop before or after the yatra.

name: Nau Devi Temple
description: Ancient temple in Jammu dedicated to nine forms of Goddess Durga. Important Navratri celebrations draw large crowds.
